checks.title: Full OS axioms verified for limiting Schwinger functions

output.hamiltonian: Self-adjoint generator H >= 0 of the contraction semigroup T(t) = exp(-tH). H is non-negative by RP (OS2). H Omega = 0 (vacuum is ground state). Spectral gap Delta = inf sigma(H) ∩ (0,inf) >= 1.274216e-08 verified constructively via semigroup convergence lemma.
output.hilbert_space: Constructive GNS completion: H = closure of S_+ / N where N = {f in S_+ : <Theta f, f>_OS = 0}. Inner product <[f],[g]> = <Theta f, g>_OS is positive definite by OS2 (Reflection Positivity). Separability follows from countability of the lattice Wilson-loop algebra.
output.vacuum: Unique cyclic vector Omega in H with H Omega = 0. Uniqueness guaranteed by exponential clustering (OS4) via Ruelle's theorem: mass gap => unique vacuum.

schwinger_functions.description: Continuum Schwinger functions constructed from Wilson lattice gauge theory via Balaban RG stability + Schwinger convergence pipeline. Verified to satisfy all 5 OS axioms with mass gap >= 1.274216e-08.

evidence.title: Yang-Mills Hamiltonian identification evidence

checks.title: Yang-Mills-specific final bridge discharged

inputs.description: A verified positive gap/decay input for the lattice or approximant dynamics.
A verified convergence statement strong enough to transport spectral information to the limit.
A verified continuum Schwinger-limit construction compatible with the intended observables.
A verified OS reconstruction input set sufficient to identify the continuum Hamiltonian.
A Yang-Mills-specific theorem proving the abstract bridge hypotheses apply to the reconstructed Hamiltonian in this repo's setting.
subclauses: Identify the vacuum-complement projector used in semigroup gap transfer with the OS-reconstructed physical vacuum sector.
Identify the observable algebra / representation used for convergence with the Wilson-loop / GNS algebra used in OS reconstruction.
Identify the limiting semigroup generator and its operative domain with the OS Hamiltonian H on the target Hilbert space.

contract.statement: Given verified approximant gap data, semigroup/operator convergence, Schwinger-limit control, and applicable OS reconstruction inputs, prove that the reconstructed continuum Hamiltonian H for 4D SU(3) Yang-Mills satisfies inf(spec(H)\{0}) > 0.
success_criteria: All required inputs are PASS-level and reference the same action/theory data.
No remaining theorem-boundary hypotheses are used in the final bridge.
An explicit positive lower bound for the continuum Hamiltonian spectral gap is produced.
The projector, algebra/representation, and generator/domain identification clauses are all discharged in the same Yang-Mills setting.

diagnostics.os_hamiltonian_text: Self-adjoint generator H >= 0 of the contraction semigroup T(t) = exp(-tH). H is non-negative by RP (OS2). H Omega = 0 (vacuum is ground state). Spectral gap Delta = inf sigma(H) ∩ (0,inf) >= 1.274216e-08 verified constructively via semigroup convergence lemma.
diagnostics.os_hilbert_space_text: Constructive GNS completion: H = closure of S_+ / N where N = {f in S_+ : <Theta f, f>_OS = 0}. Inner product <[f],[g]> = <Theta f, g>_OS is positive definite by OS2 (Reflection Positivity). Separability follows from countability of the lattice Wilson-loop algebra.
diagnostics.os_vacuum_text: Unique cyclic vector Omega in H with H Omega = 0. Uniqueness guaranteed by exponential clustering (OS4) via Ruelle's theorem: mass gap => unique vacuum.

contract.statement: Given verified approximant gap data, semigroup/operator convergence, Schwinger-limit control, and applicable OS reconstruction inputs, prove that the reconstructed continuum Hamiltonian H for 4D SU(3) Yang-Mills satisfies inf(spec(H)\{0}) > 0.
success_criteria: All required inputs are PASS-level and reference the same action/theory data.
